### 📝 Notes

Today
- [x] let's implement more, but also pause bruh

So the focus for the next release is:
- documentation improvements where there are gaps + making the docs follow the code if there was anything missing + fixing docs: docs folder should not have junk in it when we do a release the release flow should take all the junk and distribute it to it's correct place
- bugfixes as they come up during playtest
- more and better content: building on what we currently have + polishing the current 2 campaigns and making them a bit longer and more interesting
- tooling and refactoring: improving the existing tools and refactoring them into better structure (e.g the content linter, the mod portal, and other things: what can be merged or moved into some kind of buildtime script we do that)
    - e.g I would like the perf thing to be it's own crate with a binary that generates HTML with all the details and presents them nicely
    - I think the meta asset thing can be a Python script we hook up at build time if needed
    - the mod portal can also be just a Python script - since we will have an API that will host the mods it will be different and we will scrap the mod portal .rs code when we get there and build an API
    - I would like the mod content tool to also generate some kind of report (when you do audit for a single mod for instance) but it's a cool tool it shows where the error happens and stuff like that
    - also maybe a dedicated page (maybe in the readme it's worth updating the readme) with how to use tools and how to run each specific thing in the project and a "HOW TO" section (can describe minimal things and point to the docs/wiki for a detailed explanation, but the readme should give you the bare minimum you should know such that you can get started)
- no new features in code for the game itself for v0.7.0 just maintanance and polishing
